title: fatoua pilosa subvar. globulifera
intro: Fatoua pilosa subvar. globulifera, also known as the globe mallow or creeping mallows, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Malvaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Fatoua pilosa subvar. globulifera include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, while also providing relief from pain and reducing inflammation. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from its leaves and roots, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
